article.headline: Typhoon Bavi makes landfall in eastern China as more than 1 million are evacuated
article.excerpt_note_pt: Este é o trecho inicial da matéria, reproduzido palavra por palavra, sem modificações. A matéria completa (com as seções sobre Filipinas, Taiwan e Japão) está na fonte.
paragraphs: BEIJING (AP) — Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China's eastern province of Zhejiang late on Saturday night and was expected to gradually weaken, according to China's national weather center.

Bavi previously brought strong winds and rain to Japan's southern islands and Taiwan. It was the second typhoon to impact China in just over a week's time. The first, Maysak, made landfall in southern China on July 3.

Chinese authorities have evacuated more than 1.7 million people as of Saturday and issued high alerts while eastern China braced for Bavi, which had maximum sustained winds of 144 kph (89 mph) near its center.
After passing north of Taiwan on Saturday and making landfall in the coastal city of Yuhuan in Zhejiang, Bavi is expected to move northwestward inland, the National Meteorological Center said.

Earlier, at least 17 people were killed in the southern Philippines, mostly due to landslides that were set off by seasonal monsoon rains that Bavi intensified before the typhoon blew away toward Taiwan, Philippine officials said Saturday.

Authorities in Zhejiang had evacuated more than 1.7 million people by Saturday morning, the official Xinhua News Agency said. By noon, Shanghai, also on China's eastern coast, relocated around 34,000 residents from high-risk areas, according to Xinhua.

Southeastern Chinese cities near the coast prepared for the impacts. In Ningde in Fujian province, more than 3,700 people were relocated from high-risk onshore areas as of Friday evening, Xinhua said. Authorities in Fujian province placed over 17,000 emergency rescue workers on standby.

China's weather center issued an orange typhoon alert, the second-highest on a four-tier level, with many schools and ferry services suspended. Hundreds of flights have been canceled, and some high-speed railway services halted.
The center on Saturday also issued the first red alert for rainstorms of the year, according to state broadcaster CCTV.

Chinese authorities said Saturday they have allocated 40 million yuan ($5.9 million) in central natural disaster relief funds to support Zhejiang and Fujian provinces' typhoon prevention and emergency rescue and relief efforts.

vocabulary.meaning_en: (of a storm) to reach land after moving over the sea
vocabulary.meaning_pt: atingir a costa, chegar à terra (falando de tempestades e furacões)
vocabulary.example: Typhoon Bavi made landfall in China's eastern province of Zhejiang late on Saturday night.

vocabulary.meaning_en: to move people away from a dangerous place to a safe one
vocabulary.meaning_pt: evacuar; retirar pessoas de um lugar perigoso
vocabulary.example: Chinese authorities have evacuated more than 1.7 million people as of Saturday.

vocabulary.meaning_en: to prepare yourself for something unpleasant that is going to happen
vocabulary.example: …eastern China braced for Bavi, which had maximum sustained winds of 144 kph.

vocabulary.meaning_en: winds that blow at a constant speed over a period of time
vocabulary.meaning_pt: ventos sustentados, constantes (medida oficial da força de uma tempestade)
vocabulary.example: …which had maximum sustained winds of 144 kph (89 mph) near its center.

vocabulary.meaning_en: a mass of earth or rock that slides down a mountain or slope
vocabulary.example: …mostly due to landslides that were set off by seasonal monsoon rains.

vocabulary.meaning_en: to cause something to start happening
vocabulary.example: …landslides that were set off by seasonal monsoon rains that Bavi intensified.

vocabulary.meaning_en: ready to act immediately if needed
vocabulary.example: Authorities in Fujian province placed over 17,000 emergency rescue workers on standby.

vocabulary.meaning_en: money used to help people after a disaster
vocabulary.meaning_pt: fundos de ajuda emergencial, verba de socorro
vocabulary.example: …40 million yuan ($5.9 million) in central natural disaster relief funds.

reading.warmup_pt: Antes de ler: você já passou por uma tempestade muito forte? Enquanto lê, repare COMO a China se preparou para o tufão — quais ações concretas as autoridades tomaram antes de ele chegar.

questions.q: Where did Typhoon Bavi make landfall?
questions.explain_pt: O 1º parágrafo diz que Bavi "made landfall in China's eastern province of Zhejiang", na cidade costeira de Yuhuan. Filipinas, Japão e Taiwan foram afetados antes, no caminho do tufão.

questions.q: What was expected to happen to Bavi after landfall?
options: It was expected to become stronger
It was expected to gradually weaken
It was expected to return to the sea
It was expected to hit Japan again
questions.explain_pt: "…and was expected to gradually weaken" — era esperado que ele enfraquecesse aos poucos. "Weaken" = ficar mais fraco (weak + -en).

questions.q: Bavi was the first typhoon to hit China this month.
questions.explain_pt: Falso. Foi o SEGUNDO em pouco mais de uma semana: "The first, Maysak, made landfall in southern China on July 3."

questions.q: How many people had authorities in Zhejiang evacuated by Saturday morning?
questions.explain_pt: Cuidado com os números! 1,7 milhão = evacuados em Zhejiang; 34.000 = realocados em Xangai; 3.700 = realocados em Ningde; 17.000 = socorristas de prontidão em Fujian.

questions.q: Schools and ferry services continued to operate normally during the alert.
questions.explain_pt: Falso. O texto diz: "…with many schools and ferry services suspended" — suspensos. Além disso, centenas de voos foram cancelados e trens de alta velocidade, interrompidos ("halted").

listening.intro_pt: Primeiro, ouça a notícia inteira SEM ler o texto — use a velocidade lenta (0.75x) se precisar e ouça quantas vezes quiser. Depois responda às perguntas abaixo. Só mostre o texto no final, para conferir.

questions.q: According to the report, when did Bavi make landfall?
questions.explain_pt: "…made landfall … late on Saturday night" — tarde da noite de sábado. July 3 foi o tufão anterior, Maysak.

questions.q: What TWO alerts did China's weather center issue?
options: A red typhoon alert and an orange rain alert
An orange typhoon alert and a red alert for rainstorms
questions.explain_pt: Pegadinha clássica de listening: foi um alerta LARANJA para o tufão (o 2º mais alto) e o primeiro alerta VERMELHO do ano para CHUVAS (rainstorms).

questions.q: How much money did Chinese authorities allocate in disaster relief funds?
questions.explain_pt: "…allocated 40 million yuan ($5.9 million) in central natural disaster relief funds…". Em listening, treine ouvir números grandes: forty million.

dictation.after: in China's eastern province of Zhejiang late on Saturday night and was expected to gradually weaken, according to China's national weather center.
dictation.hint_pt: substantivo: o momento em que a tempestade chega à terra

dictation.after: more than 1.7 million people as of Saturday and issued high alerts while eastern China braced for Bavi, which had maximum sustained winds of 144 kph (89 mph) near its center.
dictation.hint_pt: verbo no particípio: retirar pessoas de uma área de risco

dictation.after: , and some high-speed railway services halted.
dictation.hint_pt: particípio: voos que não vão mais acontecer (grafia americana com um L)

writing.title: Uma mensagem em meio ao tufão
writing.task_pt: Imagine que você está em Xangai a trabalho e o tufão Bavi acabou de chegar. Escreva uma mensagem em inglês (e-mail ou WhatsApp) para um amigo contando a situação. Use pelo menos DUAS informações reais da notícia e o present perfect pelo menos DUAS vezes.
writing.prompt_en: You are in Shanghai on a work trip during Typhoon Bavi. Write a short message to a friend telling them what is happening and how you are. Use at least two facts from today's article and the present perfect at least twice.
checklist_pt: Usei duas informações reais da notícia (números, lugares ou ações das autoridades).
Usei o present perfect pelo menos duas vezes (ex.: have evacuated, has been canceled).
Usei pelo menos duas palavras do vocabulário de hoje.
Revisei pontuação, maiúsculas e a concordância (he/she has, they have).
writing.model_answer: Hi Marcos! I'm safe, don't worry, but things here are intense. Typhoon Bavi has just made landfall south of Shanghai, and the city has relocated about 34,000 residents from high-risk areas. My flight home has been canceled, and some high-speed trains have stopped too, so I'm staying at the hotel until Monday. I've never seen rain like this in my life! Authorities have put thousands of rescue workers on standby, so I feel okay. I'll text you tomorrow. Take care!
writing.model_note_pt: Repare no exemplo: "has just made landfall", "has been canceled", "have put … on standby" — o present perfect conecta o evento recente ao momento presente. (Este texto é apenas um modelo de escrita criado para a aula; ele não faz parte da notícia.)

shadow_sentences: Bavi previously brought strong winds and rain to Japan's southern islands and Taiwan.
It was the second typhoon to impact China in just over a week's time.
Southeastern Chinese cities near the coast prepared for the impacts.
Hundreds of flights have been canceled, and some high-speed railway services halted.

questions.q_en: What happened in China this weekend? Summarize the news in your own words.
questions.support_pt: Estruture a resposta: What happened? → Where? → What did the authorities do? Tente falar por 45–60 segundos sem parar.

questions.q_en: In your opinion, did the Chinese authorities react well? Why or why not?
questions.support_pt: Frases úteis: "I think they reacted quickly because…", "They have evacuated…", "On the other hand…"

questions.q_en: Have you ever experienced extreme weather? What did you do?
questions.support_pt: Use o present perfect para a experiência ("I have seen…", "I have never experienced…") e o past simple para os detalhes ("It happened in 2022, when…").

speaking.tip_pt: Grave suas respostas no celular e ouça depois: você mesmo vai notar pequenos erros de pronúncia e gramática — é um dos jeitos mais eficazes de melhorar o speaking.

grammar.title: Present perfect nas notícias — "have evacuated", "have been canceled"
grammar.point_pt:
Jornalistas usam o present perfect (have/has + particípio) para falar de ações recentes cujos efeitos continuam agora, sem dizer a hora exata: "Authorities have evacuated 1.7 million people" — a evacuação é recente e a situação continua. Quando a notícia informa quando algo aconteceu, ela muda para o past simple: "Bavi made landfall late on Saturday night", "The first, Maysak, made landfall on July 3."

Na voz passiva, o present perfect fica have/has been + particípio: "Hundreds of flights have been canceled" — o importante é o que aconteceu com os voos, não quem cancelou.

Resumo: ação recente + efeito no presente, sem hora exata → present perfect. Momento definido (Saturday, July 3, yesterday) → past simple.
examples: Chinese authorities <mark>have evacuated</mark> more than 1.7 million people as of Saturday…
Hundreds of flights <mark>have been canceled</mark>, and some high-speed railway services halted.
Chinese authorities said Saturday they <mark>have allocated</mark> 40 million yuan ($5.9 million) in central natural disaster relief funds…
Typhoon Bavi <mark>made landfall</mark> in China's eastern province of Zhejiang <u>late on Saturday night</u>… (past simple: o texto diz QUANDO)

exercises.q: Which sentence uses the present perfect correctly?
options: Authorities have evacuate more than 1.7 million people.
Authorities has evacuated more than 1.7 million people.
Authorities have evacuated more than 1.7 million people.
Authorities evacuated have more than 1.7 million people.
exercises.explain_pt: Estrutura: have/has + particípio. "Authorities" é plural → HAVE evacuated. A opção A usa o verbo base (evacuate) e a B usa "has" com sujeito plural.

exercises.q: Why does the report say "Hundreds of flights HAVE BEEN CANCELED" instead of "Airlines have canceled hundreds of flights"?
options: Because the important information is what happened to the flights, not who did it
Because 'have been' means the action happened long ago
exercises.explain_pt: É a voz passiva no present perfect (have been + particípio): o foco vai para os voos cancelados. Quem cancelou é óbvio ou pouco importante — padrão típico de manchete e notícia.

exercises.q: Complete with the correct form: "The weather center ___ a red alert for rainstorms this year."
exercises.explain_pt: "The weather center" é singular → HAS + particípio (issued). No texto original aparece no past simple ("issued") porque o repórter marca o tempo com "on Saturday".

exercises.after: allocated $5.9 million to help the affected provinces.
exercises.hint_pt: "the government" é singular: has ou have?
particípio de "cancel" (as duas grafias são aceitas)
